response: This code implements the optimized Sieve of Eratosthenes algorithm to generate all prime numbers between 0 and a given input integer n: it initializes a boolean array to track primality, marks 0 and 1 as non-prime, then for each candidate prime from 2 up to the square root of n, marks all multiples of that candidate starting at i² (skipping already processed smaller multiples) as non-prime, before returning the full filtered list of primes up to n.
